item.title: Police believe remains may belong to missing grandfather | SBS News in Easy English 24 February 2026
item.description: A five minute bulletin for English language learners

item.title: How much you need for a comfortable retirement & AI fears continues to spook investors
item.description: SBS Finance Editor Ricardo Gonçalves speaks with Mike Jenneke from UBS Wealth Management about the day's market action including investors’ renewed AI fears; plus Stephanie Youssef talks with Jame Koval from ASFA to find out how much you need for a comfortable retirement.

item.title: I've been goth for almost 40 years. Here's what I learned about fitting in
item.description: Emma Baird entered the Perth goth scene in the late 1980s. She believes it was pivotal in relieving her feeling of social isolation.

item.title: Are you on track to retire comfortably? Australia's new super benchmark released
item.description: Despite the increased benchmark, a superannuation expert says one group of Australians are right on track for a comfortable retirement.

item.title: Full steam ahead: Federal Government launches plans for first high-speed Sydney to Newcastle train service
item.description: The Federal Government has moved to the next stage of Australia's inaugural high-speed rail project, launching a two-year development phase for the Sydney-to-Newcastle link. Construction is slated to begin in 2028. While the project faces a nearly $90 billion price tag and public scepticism over its long history of delays, proponents argue the 180-kilometre network will eventually transform regional housing and productivity across the entire East Coast.

item.title: The UK's new entry rules are here. What do they mean for you?
item.description: UK citizens will need a valid British or Irish passport to enter the country. The decision has spelled chaos for Australian dual citizens.

item.title: The UK's new entry rules are here. What do they mean for you?
item.description: UK citizens will need a valid British or Irish passport to enter the country. The decision has spelled chaos for Australian dual citizens.

item.title: SBS News In Easy English 25 February 2026
item.description: A five minute bulletin for English language learners

item.title: AI blamed for mass Wisetech job cuts & Woolies CEO on reputation rebuild
item.description: Hear from Wisetech CEO Zubin Appoo as he explains why 2000 jobs will go at the company as AI hits its workforce; SBS Finance Editor Ricardo Gonçalves speaks with Francesco De Stradis from Ord Minnett to find out the implications for the broader market; plus Woolworths CEO Amanda Bardwell on how the supermarket operator is winning back consumer trust.

item.title: Poet Evelyn Araluen wins Victoria's highest literary award for 'uncompromising' collection
item.description: The Goorie/Koori poet has won the $100,000 Victorian Prize for Literature for her poetry collection The Rot.

item.title: Poet Evelyn Araluen wins Victoria's highest literary award for 'uncompromising' collection
item.description: The Goorie/Koori poet has won the $100,000 Victorian Prize for Literature for her poetry collection The Rot.

item.title: Syrian Kurdish official fears IS-linked cohort will 'become radicals' if not taken in
item.description: An official overseeing a Syrian detention camp says Australia must take responsibility for the 34 women and children living there.

item.title: Want your property 'pinch me' moment? Here's how long it will take
item.description: The time it takes to save a deposit to buy a home has blown out, according to a new report.

item.title: Israel responsible for two-thirds of record 129 journalist deaths in 2025, watchdog finds
item.description: Most journalist deaths last year were from armed conflict, making it the second consecutive record-breaking year for fatalities.

item.title: New website and app to safeguard international students in Australia
item.description: For international students studying in Australia, accessing healthcare is often seen as too expensive - or too confusing to navigate. But a new initiative - designed by international students FOR international students - aims to make services easier to find, while breaking down taboos around sexual and mental health.

item.title: 'Dial down the rhetoric' PM says, amid jump in threats to MPs
item.description: The emergency evacuation of the Prime Minister from his Canberra home due to a targeted bomb threat has prompted warnings of a heightened security environment, where online hostility is increasingly manifesting as high-harm physical threats. It follows comments by the Federal Police Commissioner late last year, noting a trend of escalating violence against public officials.

item.title: South African men allegedly lured to fight for Russia in Ukraine return home
item.description: South African President Cyril Ramaphosa thanked his Russian counterpart, Vladimir Putin, for helping to secure the group's return.

item.title: What Aussies should do after $450 energy bill 'shock absorber' axed
item.description: The first quarterly energy bills of the year are arriving — and you might notice something is missing.

item.title: Talks continue between Iran and the US | SBS News in Easy English 26 February 2026
item.description: A five minute bulletin for English language learners

item.title: The Chinese dance group at the centre of the Albanese bomb threat — and its link to Trump
item.description: A threat that forced the prime minister to temporarily evacuate his official residence was directed at the Shen Yun company.

item.title: How the US created a vacuum for the IS-linked cohort trying to return to Australia
item.description: The US is halting support of two IS-linked camps in Syria, saying their help "cannot last forever".

item.title: Ukraine’s grim anniversary, ‘El Mencho’ killed & SOTU, Trump’s very, very long speech
item.description: Can Ukraine hold the line as US support wavers going into a fifth year of fighting Russia’s full-scale invasion? Plus, an explosion of violence in Mexico after the killing of brutal cartel boss ‘El Mencho’ - will calm be restored in time for the World Cup? The US Supreme Court takes down Trump’s tariffs and POTUS uses his high-stakes State of the Union address to accuse Somalis of fraud

item.title: A global spectacle, a local crisis: Cartel violence and Mexico's World Cup moment
item.description: Cartel violence has swept through parts of Mexico in recent days, raising questions over the country's ability to host football's biggest tournament.

item.title: Nvidia results spur AI tech recovery & why most borrowers will cope with rate rise
item.description: Hear from Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang on why he thinks huge capital expenditure on AI technology will continue as SBS Finance Editor Ricardo Gonçalves speaks with Heath Moss from HLM Investments about the days market action including a recovery in tech stocks and a look at Qantas' half-year results. Plus Erin Kitson from S&P Global explains how borrowers are likely to cope with rising interest rates.

item.title: 'Tip of the iceberg': Australia faces a rising threat
item.description: In a more polarised world, political threats and violence are on the rise — and Australia is not immune.

item.title: Your guide to the Women's Asian Cup 2026, as the Matildas chase redemption
item.description: The Matildas will be hoping for a better result at the home tournament, after a shock early exit last time around.

item.title: Dateline returns with stories of the people caught in Trump's immigration crackdown
item.description: Dateline's new season sees reporter Darren Mara meet ordinary Minnesotans who've found themselves at the heart of US President Donald Trump's crackdown on immigration.

item.title: I run a gay sauna in Australia. You'd be surprised who comes through the door
item.description: Once stigmatised — and illegal — gay saunas like the one Glenn McNamara runs hold a special place in LGBTQI+ history.

item.title: Last-minute apology for 'racist' posts 'doesn't change anything' for NT administrator, Elder says
item.description: The incoming administrator's apology for widely criticised social media posts has not been accepted by some of his critics.

item.title: 'Fractured trust': Veteran Navy officer sacked from multicultural board after Israel posts
item.description: Mona Shindy was dumped by Multicultural NSW from its advisory board this week after she shared posts on LinkedIn about Israel and Gaza.

item.title: Sussan Ley won't return to parliament for farewell but is 'proud' of leadership milestone
item.description: Becoming the first woman to lead any federal Opposition in Australia was "a milestone for all women to be proud of", Ley said.

item.title: 'Lured in': The Russian recruitment scam sending Africans to fight in Ukraine
item.description: With the Russian Army losing an estimated 30 to 50,000 ((30,000 to 50,000)) soldiers per month, Russia is reportedly using recruitment scams to lure in men from across Africa. Offered enticing salaries and told to sign a contract in Russian, officials say economic incentives and the exploitation of vulnerable people are driving recruitment.

item.title: New measures to protect children in childcare | SBS News in Easy English 27 February 2025
item.description: A five minute bulletin for English language learners

item.title: Magda Szubanski says she's in remission after completing chemotherapy for cancer
item.description: The comedian said she was "eternally grateful" to her medical team and those who've wished her well over the past nine months.

item.title: A troubling new record as Australians' financial woes surge
item.description: Last year, a record 111,373 Australians lodged complaints with the financial watchdog — a 14 per cent rise from 2024.

item.title: Pakistan says it's in 'open war' with Afghanistan after launching airstrikes in multiple cities
item.description: It comes after months of clashes between the two neighbours along the frontier.

item.title: AI-related job cuts continues as Block slashes 4,000 roles & Australia Post's digital transformation
item.description: Hear from Block CEO Jack Dorsey as he explains why the company is cutting nearly half of its workforce; SBS Finance Editor Ricardo Gonçalves speaks with Omkar Joshi from Opal Capital Management about the future of AI and the impact on stocks. Plus Australia Post CEO Paul Graham details the group's digital transformation plan as it records a fall in profit.

item.title: The 'ISIS bride' who was rendered stateless and couldn't return home
item.description: In recent years, Shamima Begum said she lived in a dusty tent, unable to leave, with an "indefinitely" similar future ahead of her.

item.title: Ali's friend died awaiting a visa. A new pathway offers hope to LGBTQI+ refugees like him
item.description: With resettlement options collapsing worldwide, a new community-led initiative will help LGBTQI+ people get to safety in Australia.

item.title: The detectors on guard 15 years after New Zealand's devastating earthquake in Christchurch
item.description: Fifteen years after a devastating earthquake in New Zealand left 185 people dead, scientists are on guard, ready to spot an impending disaster. In the control rooms at Earth Sciences New Zealand headquarters, seismologists are continuously analysing data from a network of 700 detectors looking for warning signs of an imminent quake.

item.title: Many fleeing war are haunted by painful memories. This Australian hotline takes their calls for help
item.description: A hotline offering multi-lingual support for refugees who’ve fled war, violence and persecution has expanded nationwide. As global conflicts grind on, experts say demand is soaring. A warning: the following story contains details that may distress some listeners.

item.title: Many fleeing war are haunted by painful memories. This Australian hotline takes their calls for help
item.description: A hotline offering multi-lingual support for refugees who’ve fled war, violence and persecution has expanded nationwide. As global conflicts grind on, experts say demand is soaring. A warning: the following story contains details that may distress some listeners.

item.title: Queen Mary to visit Uluru and her Tasmanian hometown during state visit next month
item.description: Queen Mary of Denmark is bringing a large green energy business delegation with her when she and King Frederik visit next month.

item.title: Trump raises prospect of 'friendly takeover' of Cuba
item.description: US President Trump said he had been hearing about the country's woes since he was a little boy.

item.title: As the threat of war heightens distress, help may be just a phone call away
item.description: A national hotline offers multilingual support for refugees who've fled war and violence. As global conflict continues, demand is soaring.

item.title: 'We're f--ked': The people grappling with the aftermath of Trump's immigration crackdown
item.description: In Minnesota, ICE agents have left behind a trail of trauma, with members of the Somali community still living in hiding.

item.title: The planets align for Ecstatica Galactica at Sydney Mardi Gras 2026
item.description: Thousands have attended this year's Sydney Gay and Lesbian Mardi Gras parade, 48 years since the first gay rights parade in Australia in 1978. Australia's biggest celebration of the LGBTIQ-plus community took place days after an announcement that the historic parade route will be added to Australia's National Heritage List.

item.title: INTERVIEW: How to cope with rising power prices
item.description: A new survey by financial comparison website CanStar has found that many Australian households are currently worried or have concerns about future energy bill payments as federal rebates run out this year. This is in addition to an underlying price increase in household electricity bills. Over the last month, they've risen by approximately 4.5% according to the Australian Bureau of Statistics. CanStar's Data Insights Director, Sally Tindall spoke to SBS's Cameron Carr about what the latest data is showing.

item.title: A new community-led initiative offers support to vulnerable asylum seekers
item.description: Despite record numbers of displaced people worldwide, resettlement options are facing a critical decline - global impacts including shrinking legal pathways, slashing of admission numbers and funding cuts to agencies. But for the first time, a new community-led initiative will support LGBTIQ+ refugees to apply for Australia’s humanitarian program - offering dedicated support for priority processing.

item.title: US, Israel launch coordinated attacks on Iran
In pictures: Colour, protest and pride at this year's Sydney Mardi Gras
item.description: Chants of both pride and protest echoed around Sydney as thousands of people marched along Oxford Street for the annual Mardi Gras parade.

item.title: In pictures: Colour, protest and pride at this year's Sydney Mardi Gras
item.description: Chants of both pride and protest echoed around Sydney as thousands of people marched along Oxford Street for the annual Mardi Gras parade.

item.title: 'They all had the same request': Strikes hit Fariba's hometown in Iran. Then came the messages
item.description: Iranian-Australians describe a "mixed bag" of emotions after supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, was killed in US-Israeli strikes.

item.title: Were the Israeli-US strikes on Iran legal under international law? These experts say no
item.description: The government's refusal to publicly assess the legality of the strikes risks "trashing" international law, an expert says.

item.title: Delta Goodrem to represent Australia at Eurovision 2026
item.description: Australia will send a huge household name to compete for the 70th Eurovision title in Vienna.

item.title: Delta Goodrem to represent Australia at Eurovision 2026
item.description: Australia will send a huge household name to compete for the 70th Eurovision title in Vienna.

item.title: 'We're grounded': Australian family's regret over passports trapped in 'nightmare' limbo
item.description: Gemma has had to send physical passports, birth certificates and a marriage licence overseas as part of new entry laws.

item.title: Who is leading Iran now — and how will a new supreme leader be chosen?
item.description: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei is dead, leaving Iran at war and without its most powerful figure. Here's how the system will choose his successor.

item.title: Will a 'survival pact' in Iran keep the regime alive after Khamenei?
item.description: The death of Iran's supreme leader Ali Khamenei raises serious questions about the future of the regime and its clerical rulers.

item.title: Trump says the US strikes on Iran could last up to four weeks | SBS News in Easy English 2 March 2026
item.description: A five minute bulletin for English language learners

item.title: Australia a tale of two housing markets with the largest gains in unexpected places
item.description: Home values in Sydney and Melbourne have flatlined while mid-sized capitals are posting bumper gains.

item.title: When Australians can get the best view of the 'eerie' blood moon
item.description: A rare 'blood moon' will be visible over Australia soon. Here's when to look up.

item.title: $1 a litre more: Three oil price scenarios and how they'd hit Australians
item.description: Australians' wallets will be hit harder the longer the war goes on — and not just at the pump, economists have warned.

item.title: Major parties throw bipartisan support behind US-Israeli offensive against Iran
item.description: There is bipartisan support in Australian politics for a joint US-Israeli offensive dubbed 'Operation Epic Fury', despite warnings of a major breach of international law. While the government backs the action to prevent a nuclear Iran, critics and legal experts question the legality of the strikes and Australia’s potential intelligence role.

item.title: Pauline Hanson censured over remarks there are no 'good Muslims'
item.description: The One Nation leader was criticised after making the inflammatory comments during an interview last month.

item.title: Travel insurance exclusions Australians must know amid flight chaos
item.description: Travellers are being warned war exclusions could limit their cover — even if their itinerary only includes a stopover.

item.title: Oil, gold stocks lift ASX amid Iran conflict; AI job loss fears
item.description: US and Israel’s offensive in Iran has rattled global markets. However, the Australian sharemarket reversed earlier losses, as rising commodity prices saw investors pile into energy and gold stocks, offsetting losses in other sectors. For more on this, Stephanie Youssef spoke with David Walsh, analyst at Sarto Advisory. Plus, a third of Australian workers surveyed think their jobs will disappear in the next five years because of AI. Amelia O'Carrigan, Ranstad Director of Public Sector and Business Support spoke to Stephanie Youssef about this, and the emerging generational divide.

item.title: Delta Goodrem ready to 'eclipse' the Eurovision stage
item.description: One of the country's most commercially successful and globally recognised performers has been chosen to represent Australia at this year's Eurovision song contest in Vienna. Delta Goodrem will take centre-stage at the 70th edition of the contest. It's a milestone year for the competition, and one which has become clouded by controversy.

item.title: Iran's 'weaknesses' have long been clear. So why a major escalation?
item.description: These are the factors that may have influenced the United States and Israel's deadly attack against Iran.

item.title: 'Change has a price': Iranian eyewitnesses speak from the shadows of war
item.description: Messages received by SBS News capture the mood inside Iran as some rejoice over the killing of its supreme leader, while others mourn.

item.title: Death toll from attack in South Sudan rises to 169, including 90 civilians
item.description: An attack by a group of unidentified men has resulted in the loss of 169 lives in South Sudan, a death toll that could rise further.

item.title: 'Reality check': Employers urged to address stubborn gender pay gap
item.description: Stubborn gender pay gaps between men and women in senior levels at work are being highlighted as data from more than 10,000 employers is published.

item.title: 'Pushed out of paradise': High migration is often blamed for the housing crisis. Is that right?
item.description: There's a "widely shared" belief that migration is a major contributor to Australia's housing crisis. What does the research say?

item.title: 'I don't get bored': Trump warns of prolonged Iran war in first address since strikes
item.description: In his first address since the United States and Israel launched joint strikes on Iran, Donald Trump says his war on Iran could last longer than anticipated. Spreading quickly throughout the region and rocking global financial markets, polls suggest most Americans are already opposed to the strikes.

item.title: INTERVIEW: How legal is the US-Israeli action in Iran?
item.description: As US President Donald Trump warns of a big wave of further attacks on Iran, the UN Security Council is under pressure to act over the US and Israel's strikes. Iran's U-N ambassador, Amir Saeid Iravani says the strikes on the weekend were unlawful - a position shared by many international law experts globally. So what are the facts? Dr Tamer Morris is a senior lecturer at the University of Sydney, and he's speaking with SBS reporter Rayane Tamer, who asked him what international law says about the Israeli/US strikes on Iran.

item.title: Israel strikes in Lebanon turn deadly as Iran conflict widens
item.description: At least 52 people have been killed and more than 150 injured in Lebanon, as Israel targets operations of the Iran-allied militant group Hezbollah, after it launched missiles and drones toward Israel.

item.title: Iran war impacts petrol prices | SBS News in Easy English 3 March 2026
item.description: A five minute bulletin for English language learners

item.title: Australia's new $2,300 visa price increase and more key changes
item.description: Besides doubling graduate visa application fees, the federal government has quietly introduced two other key changes.

item.title: Why some in Australia are mourning Iran's Ayatollah Khamenei, as criticism mounts
item.description: While for many Khamenei was the "embodiment of a repressive regime", one expert explains the religious reasons some are mourning.

item.title: Israeli President Isaac Herzog visited ASIO headquarters during Australian visit
item.description: Australia's intelligence agency has confirmed Herzog met with ASIO director Mike Burgess after senator David Pocock pressed for answers.

item.title: Gender pay gap still a problem in these high-earning industries
item.description: Across Australia, for every dollar a man earns, women on average are earning 88- point- 8 cents. The government's gender equality agency says the gender pay gap has narrowed to 11. 2 per cent. And while most sectors have seen improvements, pay gaps remain large in some male-dominated industries, such as finance, construction and mining.

item.title: 'Opportunistic': New petrol warning as treasurer orders price gouging scrutiny
item.description: The conflict in the Middle East has sparked fear of petrol price hikes in Australia.

item.title: How could a war in the Middle East impact petrol prices?
item.description: Hear RBA Governor Michele Bullock detail how the Middle East tensions and hit to oil supply may impact inflation and what it means for interest rates; SBS Finance Editor Ricardo Gonçalves speaks with Cameron Gleeson from Betashares to find out what it means for sharemarkets; plus Life360 CEO Lauren Antonoff talks through her company's results and AI future.

item.title: Australians recall 'frightening' moment plane turned back amid Iran war
item.description: Foreign Minister Penny Wong said DFAT is conducting its largest ever consular operation as flights out of the Middle East remain grounded.

item.title: 'War of choice': Even if he wins against Iran, will Donald Trump lose?
item.description: Polls suggest Americans are not keen on a protracted war with Iran — and experts say it may cost Donald Trump in the mid-term elections.

item.title: 'Absolutely horrific': UN calls for probe into deadly strike on girls' school in Iran
item.description: The UN says the bombing of an Iranian girls' school in US and Israeli attacks must be investigated.

item.title: Wong confirms stranded Australians on board first flight out of the UAE
item.description: Foreign minister Penny Wong says Australia is "doing all that we can", deploying additional teams to return people from the Middle East.

item.title: How Iran war is impacting oil and fuel prices in Australia and globally
item.description: Oil prices across the globe have surged to multi-year highs, as the war in Iran expands into a broader regional conflict. A prolonged war risks disrupting energy supply chains, particularly as key shipping routes through the Strait of Hormuz are effectively closed. Analysts say impacts are already being felt - and that has implications for the global economic outlook and inflation.

item.title: "Not Winston Churchill": Trump lashes out at the UK and Spain over response to Iran strikes
item.description: United States President Donald Trump has lashed out at European allies for what he says is a lack of support on Iran. As diplomatic ties strain over the war, Iran says the time for negotiations has passed, warning Europe not to get involved.

item.title: Military action continues in the Middle East | SBS News in Easy English 4 March 2026
item.description: A five minute bulletin for English language learners

item.title: Canadian PM out of step with Albanese after pivot on US-Israeli strikes
item.description: The Canadian prime minister has called for a diplomatic end to the War in the Middle East, warning against escalation.

item.title: Australian family flees to Lebanon's mountains amid 'merry go-round' Israeli attacks
item.description: An Australian living in Beirut says many people are scared but used to warfare in the country.

item.title: Rain-battered areas now face cyclone warning in Queensland's north
item.description: Australia's rain-battered far north is preparing to batten down the hatches again as a possible tropical cyclone looks set to cross the coast near Cairns in the coming days.

item.title: Matildas coach urges 'human compassion' for Iran football team amid striker's tears
item.description: Iranian striker Sara Didar fought back tears at a press conference ahead Thursday's Women's Asian Cup match against the Matildas.

item.title: Australian investors dump $63bn as Middle-East war stokes global inflation concerns
item.description: SBS Finance Editor Ricardo Gonçalves speaks with Harry Murphy Cruise from Oxford Economics about the impact of the Middle East war on the Australian economy, even as GDP expands at its fastest rate in almost three years. Plus Niv Dagan from Peak Asset Management on the day's sharemarket news including the market's reaction to ARN Media's decision to pull the Kyle and Jackie O show.

item.title: Inmate turned advocate wants more support for women on the outside
item.description: A First Nations advocate and activist for incarcerated women says the social contract between the Australian government and the community is broken. Tahlia Isaac, who founded 'Project:herSelf', says authorities are more interested in punishment than providing the conditions to keep people safe. She says the system needs to change and the government needs to start listening to women who have real insight into the cycle of incarceration.

item.title: $2,850 and major flight doubts: Australian stranded in Saudi Arabia's travel nightmare
item.description: Many Muslims who have travelled to Saudi Arabia for Ramadan now find themselves unable to leave, with flights largely grounded.

item.title: Top US commander says Iran strikes 'ahead of game plan' as markets dive
item.description: Despite US assurances that the war in the Middle East is ahead of schedule, markets reacted to concerns ‌of prolonged conflict in the region.

item.title: From political oblivion to poll surge: Inside Pauline Hanson's comeback
item.description: Accusations of electoral fraud, persistent immigration rhetoric and a distinct orange tinge.

item.title: I joined a Melbourne street gang when I was 13. Why I left that life behind
item.description: When Mar Manasseh Riek struggled to fit in as a first-generation Australian, he found a sense of belonging in a dangerous place.

item.title: Iran says US will 'bitterly regret' sinking ship in Indian Ocean as it trades strikes with Israel
item.description: US defence secretary Pete Hegseth says a torpedo from a US submarine has sunk an Iranian warship off the coast of Sri Lanka.

item.title: 'It was scary to sleep': Australians touch down after days stranded in Dubai
item.description: More than 200 Australians have arrived home to Sydney after being stuck in Dubai since US-Israeli strikes on Iran began on the weekend.

item.title: INTERVIEW: Penny Wong on Australia's involvement in the Iran conflict
item.description: Australia’s Foreign Affairs Minister Penny Wong has sat down with Federal Politics reporter Anna Henderson, where she was pressed on Canberra’s position and whether Australia has any involvement in the escalating global conflict following the recent attacks on Iran. The Minister addressed questions about the legal justification for the strikes, Australia’s role, and national security concerns as tensions continue to reverberate beyond the Middle East.

item.title: INTERVIEW: Is Australia providing intelligence for the US-Israel attacks on Iran?
item.description: A former intelligence officer who resigned over the Iraq War has told SBS it's indisputable that Australian intelligence is being used by Israel and the United States in their war against Iran. Referring partly to Pine Gap, the secret US-Australian intelligence base near Alice Springs, Independent Andrew Wilkie is demanding the parliament be able to scrutinise any Australian involvement in what he claims are a series of illegal strikes by western allies. Israel's top Canberra diplomat says the strikes were in self defence and complied with international law. Andrew Wilkie is talking to SBS Chief Political Correspondent Anna Henderson.

item.title: PM says military assets deployed to Middle East as Opposition seeks more details
item.description: The government had been facing questions over what support Australia will offer citizens given the scale of the Middle East crisis.

item.title: Under half of young Australians believe democracy is always a preferable form of government
item.description: While new research has found Australians are broadly supportive of democracy, many are unsatisfied with its outcomes.

item.title: Albanese sharpens stance on de-escalation in Middle East, joining Canadian PM
item.description: The prime ministers have responded to questioning about their initial statements of support for Israeli-US strikes on Iran.

item.title: "More resilient, more just": Canada and Australia to work closely amid "ruptured" rules-based global order
item.description: Australia and Canada have agreed to deepen their ties after Canada's Prime Minister urged middle powers to collaborate in the face of a ruptured ruled-based world order. Describing the two countries as strategic cousins, both Mark Carney and Anthony Albanese agreed that like-minded middle powers must work together to secure their future.

item.title: European leaders are voice concerns over war against Iran | SBS News in Easy English 5 March 2026
item.description: A five minute bulletin for English language learners

item.title: Government's controversial freedom of information reforms dropped after broad opposition
item.description: Labor argued the laws needed to be updated as they hadn't been reformed in decades, but found little support for their proposed changes.

item.title: Stranded in Dubai, Jo's only option was to watch her father's funeral on Zoom
item.description: With waves of airstrikes grounding flights across the Middle East, Jo Swain hasn't been able to make it to the UK, or Australia.

item.title: Bad Bunny's Austrailan debut drew a record 89,000 fans. These fans explain why it was such a big deal
item.description: Almost 90-thousand people have packed a Sydney stadium to see Bad Bunny’s first live concerts in Australia. It's a record for the ENGIE Stadium, underlining the global audience for music sung in Spanish, including among Western audiences that don't understand or speak the language.

item.title: Tens of thousands displaced as Israel bombs Beirut
item.description: Explosions have lit up the skies over Beirut as Israeli strikes continue to target Hezbollah positions in the Lebanese capital, widening the regional war that began after the U-S and Israeli strikes on Iran. Officials say more than 70 people have been killed in Lebanon, while tens of thousands of civilians have been forced to flee their homes.

item.title: Iran’s regime on the brink? US-Israel strikes, oil shock fears & the battle for what comes next
item.description: With the Supreme Leader killed, Iran lashes out. Will the Middle East choose sides as oil prices skyrocket and travel chaos sees thousands stranded. Plus, who could be next in line in Iran, could the US strikes encourage others into action and proof that sometimes the best defence is a very loud, slightly unhinged cuddly toy.

item.title: Why airfares could rise in coming years & US data reignites rate cut hopes
item.description: SBS Finance Editor Ricardo Gonçalves speaks with Robert Talevski from Activam about the day's market action while Stephanie Youseff discusses the ACCC's latest airports report which warns high levels of investment will be passed onto airlines which may increase airfares as a result.

item.title: 'We were already concerned': Alarm over Iranian broadcast labelling football team 'traitors'
item.description: Concerns about the safety of the Iranian team have been raised as sources visiting a hotel says they're treated like "prisoners" on tour.

item.title: Fears of 'second massacre' in Iran grow as regime takes 'revenge' on prisoners
item.description: Amid escalating conflict in Iran, human rights groups have expressed concern for the thousands of political protesters held in jail cells.

item.title: 'Euro summer' in question as experts caution Australians over travel plans
item.description: Australians may need to "seriously reconsider" travel plans in the coming months.

item.title: Your guide to the Milano Cortina 2026 Winter Paralympics
item.description: The Winter Paralympic Games will kick off this weekend. Here's everything you need to know.

item.title: Donald Trump says US wants to be involved in choosing Iran's next leader as conflict spreads
item.description: Donald Trump insists he has a say in picking Iran's next supreme leader, as the conflict reverberates throughout the region.

item.title: Kristi Noem replaced as head of US homeland security, Donald Trump announces
item.description: Kristi Noem, a high-profile member of Donald Trump's cabinet, was criticised for her approach to the immigration crackdown.

item.title: More heavy rain predicted after Far North Queensland lashed by wild weather
item.description: Emergency crews are ready across Queensland's far north coast with heavy rain, flooding and damaging winds forecast for Friday.

item.title: INTERVIEW: Indigenous Australians Minister on the parliamentary inquiry into racism
item.description: A federal parliamentary inquiry has opened into racism in Australia, with advocates calling for the probe to be not 'another exercise in diagnosis'. The Australian Human Rights Commission says the government already knows what needs to change, and the inquiry must turn that knowledge into action, with timelines and accountability. Meanwhile the federal Minister for Indigenous Australians Malandirri McCarthy is encouraging big social media platforms to make submissions to the inquiry. She's told NITV some Indigenous people are feeling scared after a string of reports of racist attacks in public. She's talking here to NITV's John Paul Janke

item.title: 'A lot of respect': Matildas swap jerseys with Iranian team after victory on Gold Coast
item.description: One Iranian player fought back tears as she discussed the situation in her home country and the war in the Middle East.

item.title: Hizb ut-Tahrir group banned in Australia under new hate speech laws, after ASIO call
item.description: The Islamist group is the first to be listed as a hate group under recently introduced legislation.

item.title: Australia-bound flights from the Middle East arriving with empty seats
item.description: Flights carrying Australians home from the Middle East are arriving with empty seats, the result of late confirmations and safety concerns.

item.title: PM confirms ADF links to conflict amid fresh flights to Australia from Middle East hub
item.description: After considerable questioning, PM confirms three ADF personnel were on a US submarine that sank an Iranian warship.

item.title: So, Ukraine does have a card: Trump looks to Zelenskyy for help combating Iranian drones
item.description: As the US-Israeli war on Iran rages on, the United States has asked Ukraine for help to fend off Iranian drone attacks in the region. Despite Donald Trump's scaled back support for Ukraine and Israel's refusal to impose sanctions on Russia, Ukraine has offered allies in the region a swap of key defence systems.

item.title: Israel's army to expand its area of control in southern Lebanon |SBS News in Easy English 6 March 2026
item.description: A five minute bulletin for English language learners

item.title: Counting underway in Nepal's general elections
item.description: Counting is underway in Nepal's general elections which were held six months after deadly anti-corruption protests toppled the government in the Himalayan nation. Nearly 19 million voters were eligible to choose who replaces the interim government in place since the September 2025 uprising. A winner is not expected to be announced until after a week.

item.title: Meet ‘Tous’ — a marsupial previously thought to be extinct for 6,000 years
item.description: There are only about 1,300 genera of living mammals worldwide. Discovering a totally new genus of mammal happens only a few times a year.

item.title: ASX’s worst day since Liberation Day; oil price shock
item.description: The S&P/ASX 200 has recorded its worst weekly drop since Donald Trump’s so-called Liberation Day tariffs shocked global markets in April last year. That’s as investors weigh the prospect of a drawn-out war in the Middle East, with the rising oil price prompting further inflation concerns. Meanwhile, the US Pentagon has formally labelled AI company Anthropic a supply chain risk, escalating its dispute over AI safeguards. For more, Stephanie Youssef spoke with capital.com senior market analyst Kyle Rodda.

item.title: 'As long as it takes': Experts predict possible outcomes of war in the Middle East
item.description: As the War in the Middle East reaches the one week mark, experts speculate on how the crisis could play out.

item.title: In a world of superyachts and fast cars, Alex Albon is just trying to stay present
item.description: The British Thai driver is used to people trying to put him in boxes. But identity doesn't quite work like that, he says.

item.title: Fears for low income families during Ramadan, as Middle East conflict threatens to send food prices higher
item.description: Families in Australia are bracing for a cost-of-living hit, as energy prices soar. Charities are increasing gifts of free food during Ramadan, to help those already struggling. SBS Arabic takes a look at how one group is trying to help.

item.title: Trump demands 'unconditional surrender' of Iran as wave of 'broad-scale' strikes hits Tehran
item.description: The US president has given Iran an ultimatum to end the war, while Israel launched "a broad-scale wave of strikes" on Tehran.

item.title: Hundreds more Australians arrive home safely from the Middle East | Midday News Bulletin 7 March 2026
item.description: Hundreds more Australians arrive home safely from the Middle East; President Trump demands 'unconditional surrender' from Iran as strikes intensify; and in sport, Cristiano Ronaldo takes a break from the Saudi Pro League for treatment in Spain.

item.title: The cost of war: How the Middle East conflict could worsen food insecurity in Australia
item.description: Long queues, scarce groceries and rising fuel bills — Australia's food insecurity may worsen, experts warn, as war in the Middle East threatens to drive up prices.

item.title: More arrive from the Middle East overnight, as others pay thousands to leave by taxi
item.description: Australians are exploring different ways to leave the Middle East amid a conflict that has "no end game".

item.title: Israel accused of targeting UN peacekeepers after three injured in south Lebanon strike
item.description: Ghana's military said that its UNIFIL battalion headquarters in southern Lebanon came under "two missile attacks".

item.title: Russia returns to Paralympic Games, 16 countries boycott opening ceremony
item.description: Ukraine said boycotting the Paralympic Winter Games' opening ceremony was "a principled decision".

item.title: Weapons companies to quadruple supplies to US as Iran war continues | Evening News Bulletin 7 March 2026
item.description: Australia urging citizens to take commercial flights home from the Middle East; Donald Trump meets with major defence manufacturers as the war continues; The Matildas getting ready for South Korea in their next Asian Cup game.

item.title: Trump threatens more strikes on Iran | Morning News Bulletin 8 March 2026
item.description: In this bulletin, Donald Trump threatens more strikes on Iran, as Iran's president apologises to Gulf nations; Rapper-turned-politician Balendra Shah defeats former prime minister Sharma KP Oli in Nepal's election; and in Formula One, George Russell secures pole position for the Australian Grand Prix in Melbourne.

item.title: 'People confuse China's policy': Behind Russian, Chinese view on war in the Middle East
item.description: Russia, China and Iran have had close relationships for decades, but Beijing and Moscow have yet offered any direct support for Tehran.

item.title: Shantanu was shot during Nepal's gen Z protests. What's next for him — and his country?
item.description: Shantanu set out to change Nepal. Instead, the gen Z protests changed him. Nepal will soon have a new government — but will it deliver real change?

item.title: Observing Ramadan in one of Australia's most remote places
item.description: More than three thousand kilometres off the Western Australian coast lie the Cocos Islands — a tiny archipelago of 27 coral islands scattered across the Indian Ocean. Just two are inhabited. One of them is Home Island, a kampong of about 460 people, where the overwhelming majority are Cocos Malays — and where Islam has shaped daily life for generations. As Western Australia Correspondent Christopher Tan reports, it is here that the month-long fast of Ramadan has reached the halfway mark on one of Australia’s most remote communities.

item.title: It's International Women's Day. Are women any safer - and what else can be done for those fleeing violence?
item.description: A warning - the following story contains elements that may distress some people. In 2025, New South Wales alone recorded the highest number of women murdered in domestic violence situations. Advocates say it's not a one-off situation - and that women's safety remains an ongoing concern.

item.title: Who is Balendra Shah, the rapper politician poised to become Nepal's next prime minister?
item.description: A newcomer party with former rapper Balendra Shah as its main candidate is heading toward a landslide victory in Nepali elections, early results suggest.

item.title: What's causing the Greek yoghurt shortage in Australian supermarkets?
item.description: Many shops are running low on Greek yoghurt. Has tzatziki suddenly become wildly popular, or is it the result of online trends?

item.title: Australia weighs up military aid to war-hit Middle East | Midday News Bulletin 8 March 2026
item.description: In this bulletin, Australia considering military aid to Middle East nations; International Women's Day marked around the world; and at the Milano Cortina Paralympics, Australian para-alpine skier Josh Hanlon suffers a head injury on day one of competition.

item.title: Australia considering military aid to Middle East, but won't deploy troops
item.description: Iran's retaliatory strikes have hit the UAE, Qatar and Bahrain, among other countries.

item.title: SBS exclusive interview with ambassadors to Australia Fiona Flood, Beate Grzeski and Betty Pavelich
item.description: Today is International Women's Day, with the theme of 'Balancing the Scales' which calls for accelerated progress toward gender equity. SBS World News Chief Political Correspondent Anna Henderson has spoken with Irish Ambassador Fiona Flood, German Ambassador Beate Grzeski, and Croatian Ambassador Betty Pavelich on their experiences as senior diplomats. The conversation covers a range of important issues, including gender equality in world diplomacy, the conflict with Iran, and the struggles women have in getting ahead in a traditionally man's world of diplomacy.

item.title: Coalition considering support for Gulf military aid proposal | Evening News Bulletin 8 March 2026
item.description: The Coalition considering Labor's proposal for Australian military assistance to Persian Gulf countries; The US embassy in Oslo hit by an explosion; Korean Australians looking to support both the North and South teams in the Asian Cup.

item.title: Trump says war may only end when Iran's leadership, military is totally destroyed
item.description: Iran's president recently said mediation efforts were underway, but Trump's comments suggest such efforts could be a moot point.

item.title: When Janelle retired, she never guessed periods would be her new passion
item.description: Crafter Janelle Dodd has built a community to educate and help people around the world with menstruation.

item.title: Government urged to let Iranian women's football team stay in Australia until safety is assured
item.description: Several community and civil society groups have sent the home affairs minister a letter expressing "grave concerns" for the team.

item.title: 'We've reached a breaking point': Is it time to tax older Australians more?
item.description: Some Australians are richer than ever, while younger cohorts are struggling. Experts say it's time for change.

item.title: Iran and Israel exchange fresh attacks as the war enters a second week | Morning News Bulletin 9 March 2026
item.description: Iran and Israel exchange fresh attacks as the war enters a second week; Residents in the Northern Territory are warned of crocodiles following record-breaking floods; and in football, the Matildas miss-out on top spot in their Asian Cup group, after a draw against South Korea.

item.title: Israeli strikes on Lebanon kill nearly 400 as Tehran names new supreme leader
item.description: As fighting escalated on day nine of the US-Israeli campaign against Iran, thick black smoke hung over Tehran on Sunday local time.

item.title: Matildas score late in draw with South Korea, losing top spot at Women’s Asian Cup
item.description: Australia have survived a Women's Asian Cup scare, scoring a late goal to avoid defeat in a 3-3 draw with South Korea.

item.title: Who is Mojtaba Khamenei, Iran's new supreme leader?
item.description: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's son Mojtaba has succeeded his late father as supreme leader, in a sign that hardliners are still firmly in charge.

item.title: 'A disturbing picture': Australia's deadliest cancer is no longer lung cancer
item.description: A fast-growing group of cancers has quietly surpassed lung cancer as Australia's biggest killer, according to a new analysis.

item.title: Ayatollah's son is Iran's new supreme leader | Midday News Bulletin 9 March 2026
item.description: The son of the late Ayatollah named as Iran's new supreme leader... oil prices eclipse $US100 per barrel... and in cricket, India beat New Zealand to become the first team to retain the men's Twenty20 World Cup title.

item.title: Meet the tech company developing tools for digital detox, as more countries enact underage social media bans
item.description: In December 2025, Australia's ban on under 16s having social media accounts on some of the world's highest profile platforms took effect. Since then, around the world, other countries have taken note, and are implementing or considering their own underage ban.

item.title: Nepal elects 35-year-old former rapper in affirmation of Gen Z movement's political power
item.description: Six months after youth-led protests in Nepal ousted the government, voters have elected 35-year-old rapper turned politician Balendra Shah. Defeating former Prime Minister KP Sharma Oli, the landslide victory of the former mayor of Kathmandu shows the lasting strength of the youth-led movement.

item.title: SBS News in Easy English 9 March 2026
item.description: A news bulletin for those with disability and those learning English

item.title: Flood alerts in Queensland and Northern Territory as residents evacuated, urged to seek high ground
item.description: Most of Queensland is on alert for flooding as a tropical low that hammered the far north continues across the southeast.

item.title: Conflict enters new phase as Iran appoints Mojtaba Khamenei as Supreme Leader
item.description: The Middle East conflict is entering a new and uncertain phase. Iran has appointed Mojtaba Khamenei as its new Supreme Leader following the killing of his father, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, as fighting with Israel intensifies. Military strikes are escalating across the region, oil prices are surging, and world leaders are urging restraint amid fears of a wider war.

item.title: Trump condemns new Iranian Supreme Leader | Evening News Bulletin 9 March 2026
item.description: Donald Trump condemns the new Iranian Supreme Leader... large parts of Queensland in danger of being flooded... and the Matildas insist travel will be no excuse in the knockout stage of the Asian Cup

item.title: 'Maximum damage': Iran's tactics in the face of 'overwhelming' US force
item.description: Iran's retaliatory strikes reflect a strategy of survival, not victory, experts say, as war in the Middle East deepens.

item.title: From Seoul to Sydney: Fans bridging two football nations amid calls to champion women's game
item.description: Australia have faced South Korea in one of the AFC Women's Asia Cup's most anticipated fixtures, surrendering the top spot in the group to South Korea, after a 3-all draw. The match delivered a crucial result at the eleventh hour for home side. As fans celebrate the rise in women's football, experts say more work needs to be done in the space.

item.title: Women across the world take to the streets to mark International Women's Day
item.description: Women across the world have taken to the streets to mark International Women's Day, calling for an end to inequality and discrimination. Some of the issues in focus - pay inequity, reproductive rights and gender-based violence.

item.title: How high could petrol prices rise? Eight ways to stretch your tank
item.description: The average price of unleaded petrol soared past $2 a litre in many parts of the country. So how can drivers save?

item.title: How governments are responding as the Middle East conflict shakes markets
item.description: Governments around the world are assessing options for minimising the economic impact the War in the Middle East may have on their respective economies.

item.title: Australia's 'bare cupboard': What it can — and can't — offer the Gulf
item.description: As Gulf states face Iranian retaliatory strikes, defence experts detail the limited assets Australia could send — and gaps it can't fill.

item.title: 'Joy' and 'relief' for Iranian women's soccer team offered new life in Australia
item.description: Home Affairs Minister Tony Burke has offered the entire Iranian women's football team humanitarian visas, saying they're welcome to stay.

item.title: Sonja asked high schoolers to debate about tradwives — and received international backlash
item.description: In Australia, are terms such as "free speech" and "cultural safety" now used in place of constructive, respectful debate?

item.title: Five women from Iran's football team under Australian protection | Morning News Bulletin 10 March 2026
item.description: Five players from Iran's women's football team are under Australian protection; World leaders take action to curb effects of surging oil prices; and in sport, the Matildas to play North Korea in the Asian Cup quarter finals.

item.title: Regional markets smashed as oil surges over US $100
item.description: Nearly $90 billion dollars has been wiped from the S&P/ASX 200, as war in Iran sent oil prices skyrocketing past US$100 a barrel for the first time since Russia invaded Ukraine in 2022; The rising oil price sparks panic buying and reports of fuel rationing at some petrol stations in Australia.

item.title: PM says Australia will deploy RAAF plane and missiles to Gulf to protect civilians
item.description: The government has responded to a request for assistance by the United Arab Emirates, deploying a high-tech spy plane to the Middle East.

item.title: PM says Australia will deploy RAAF plane and missiles to Gulf to protect civilians
item.description: The government has responded to a request for assistance by the United Arab Emirates, deploying a high-tech spy plane to the Middle East.

item.title: Australia sends jet and missiles to the Middle East | Midday News Bulletin 10 March 2026
item.description: Australia deploys a jet and missiles to defend the United Arab Emirates against Iranian attacks... Australians living in the Middle East warned to come home now if they want to leave... and a key Aussie women's cricket player rested for the tour of the West Indies

item.title: Fuelling fears: Petrol prices sway as Trump signals possible end to US-Israeli war on Iran
item.description: Global oil prices have surged and sunk as the US-Israeli war on Iran chokes major supply chains of oil and gas. With deep uncertainty and mixed messaging over the US timeframe for the war, world leaders are scrambling to ensure they're prepared for ongoing pressures.

item.title: Greek politician faces charges after saying he took drugs at Sydney Kylie Minogue concert
item.description: Ex-finance minister calls charges "politically motivated" over remarks about drug use at a concert after the 1989 Sydney Mardi Gras parade.

item.title: SBS News In Easy English 10 March 2026
item.description: A news bulletin for those learning English and those with disability

item.title: 'I've had enough': David Littleproud resigns as National Party leader
item.description: Nationals expected to select a new leader in coming days after David Littleproud quit the party's top job, saying he was "buggered".

item.title: David Littleproud suddenly quits as federal National Party leader | Evening News Bulletin 10 March 2026
item.description: David Littleproud quits as federal National Party leader... the federal government looking at support for the Iranian-Australian community in the wake of the conflict with Iran... and the Matildas say game style will matter come Friday night against North Korea

item.title: Fighting spreads across Middle East; President Trump says war on Iran is 'tremendous success'
item.description: The war involving the United States, Israel and Iran is entering a volatile new phase, with fighting spreading across the Middle East, missiles and drones striking multiple countries, global energy markets under pressure, and hundreds of thousands of civilians forced to flee their homes.

item.title: Australia sends surveillance aircraft and troops to Middle East
item.description: Australia is deploying an E7A Wedgetail surveillance aircraft and 85 personnel to the United Arab Emirates to bolster regional air defences. The government is maintaining the mission is strictly defensive, while the Greens warn it risks entangling Australia in an illegal conflict.

item.title: INTERVIEW: Facing the might of America's military, what will Iran do next?
item.description: Iran has been retaliating since coming under United States and Israeli bombardment more than a week ago, launching strikes against Israel and Gulf states allied with the US that host American military bases. Experts are describing Iran's actions as a bid for "regime survival". Associate Professor Jessica Genauer at Flinders University says the conflict can be described as "asymmetric". She's been talking to SBS's Cameron Carr.

item.title: INTERVIEW: Facing the might of America's military, what will Iran do next?
item.description: Iran has been retaliating since coming under United States and Israeli bombardment more than a week ago, launching strikes against Israel and Gulf states allied with the US that host American military bases. Experts are describing Iran's actions as a bid for "regime survival". Associate Professor Jessica Genauer at Flinders University says the conflict can be described as "asymmetric". She's been talking to SBS's Cameron Carr.

item.title: Airfares to rise as much as 30% and ASX makes partial recovery
item.description: SBS Finance Editor Ricardo Gonçalves speaks with Cirium Aviation Analyst Ellis Taylor who says airfares may rise as much as 30 per cent as jet fuel prices soar amid the Middle-East war; and Todd Hoare from LGT Wealth Management Australia Management on the markets partial recovery following yesterday's $90bn sell-off.

item.title: 'She can't make a decision': Desperate plea amid fears Iranian players are being coerced
item.description: After five players were granted asylum, concerns are mounting over whether the remaining team members can safely seek protection.

item.title: Iran says US talks 'not on the agenda', calls previous negotiations a 'bitter experience'
item.description: The Iranian deputy foreign minister says numerous countries have urged Iran to consider a ceasefire.

item.title: Qantas hikes international fares as war sends fuel costs soaring
item.description: Despite the war, high demand for international flights has seen cheaper fares selling much faster than usual.

item.title: Bundaberg residents told to evacuate as floods trigger memories of 2010 disaster
item.description: Thousands of residents have been told to evacuate amid fears rising floodwaters could reach levels not seen in the area since 2010.

item.title: Scouring the countryside for 600L of fuel: Farmer Rob has 'never seen' anything like this
item.description: Farmers warn supply disruptions could threaten Australia's food production.

item.title: US vows most ‘intense strikes’ yet on Iran | Morning News Bulletin 11 March
item.description: The US vows to deliver the most intense strikes yet against Iran; emotional scenes as Iranian women's football team players depart Australia for Iran; Knights superstar Kalyn Ponga applies to switch allegiance to New Zealand.

item.title: 15 years on, is the world better equipped to prevent a nuclear accident similar to Fukushima?
item.description: It's been 15 years since one of the worst natural disasters in history. The 2011 East Japan Earthquake and tsunami killed more than 19,000 people and displaced hundreds of thousands of people, especially those forced to evacuate following the subsequent Fukushima nuclear accident. So what lessons can be learnt from this disaster and nuclear accident in a climate of increasing global turmoil?

item.title: US and Israel launch 'most intense day of strikes' yet against Iran
item.description: Tehran residents have described the war's heaviest night of bombardment.
